This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.



#Installing Packages
```{r}
#install.packages("BiocManager", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
#BiocManager::install("phyloseq")
#install.packages("C:/Users/reves/Downloads/pairwiseAdonis-master/pairwiseAdonis-master/pairwiseAdonis", repos = NULL, type = "source")
library(dplyr)
library(devtools)
library(BiocManager)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)
library(rdist)
#library(adespatial)
#library(devtools)
#library(factoextra)
library(ggpubr)
library(stringr)
library(tibble)
library(ExpDes)
library(tidyverse)
library(plyr)
library(ggh4x)
library(indicspecies)
library(pairwiseAdonis)
#library(broom)
library(kableExtra)
library(agricolae)
library(Matrix)
library(lme4)
library(gridExtra)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
library(car)
#library(caret)
#library(VIM)
#library(geosphere)
library(corrplot)
library(emmeans)
library(effectsize)
library(readr)
library(nlme)
```

#Tijana's Color Packages
```{r}
color_palette1 <- c("darkgreen", "darkgoldenrod1", "darkgoldenrod")
color_palette2 <- c("darkgreen", "darkgoldenrod1", "darkgoldenrod", "darkred")
color_palette4 <- c("darkgreen","#DBCA69","#D57500", "#613318")
color_palette5 <- c("darkgreen","#DBCA69","#D57500", "#613318","darkred")

palette_NMDS <- c("darkred", "darkgreen", "darkorange", "burlywood3", 
             "darkblue", "pink", "lightgray", "blue", "red",
             "darkgrey", "chartreuse3", "orange") 


color_pal_alt <- c("#006400", "#8B4513", "#D2B48C")

color_values_eco <- c("#eeeeee", "darkgrey",  "darkorange","darkgreen","darkred", "burlywood", "burlywood3", "burlywood4", "tan3")


color_values_genus <- c("#eeeeee", "burlywood3", "#065535", "#99CCFF", "darkseagreen4", "burlywood4", "#660033", "#FF9966", "#CBD588", "#5F7FC7", "orange", "#DA5724", "#508578", "#CD9BCD", "darkolivegreen4", "darkred", "#673770", "#D14285", "aquamarine4", "#652926", "#8569D5", "#5E738F", "#D1A33D", "#8A7C64", "#599861", "#C84248", "#FFCC66", "#FFFF66", "#CC3300", "#999999", "burlywood2", "#CC6600")


color_values_phylum <- c("#eeeeee","burlywood3", "#99CCFF", "#065535", "darkseagreen4", "#3333CC", "#660033", "#FF9966", "#CC6600", "#CBD588", "#5F7FC7", "orange", "#DA5724", "#508578", "#CD9BCD", "darkolivegreen4", "darkred", "#673770", "#D14285", "aquamarine4", "#652926", "#8569D5", "#5E738F", "#D1A33D", "#8A7C64", "#599861", "#C84248", "#FFCC66", "#FFFF66", "#CC3300", "#999999")


colors_geneco <- c("#eeeeee",
                   "darkgrey", "gray","#83929F",
                   
                   "chartreuse4",
                   "darkgreen","#003300",
                   "darkolivegreen", "darkseagreen4",
                   "olivedrab", 
                   "mediumseagreen", "springgreen3",
                   "springgreen4", "palegreen4",
                   "darkseagreen2","paleturquoise4",
                   "darkred",
                   "burlywood3", 
                   
                   "cornsilk1", 
                   "chocolate4",
                   "lightgoldenrod", 
                   "sandybrown",
                   "tan3", "tan4",
                   "rosybrown4",                "wheat1", 
                   "tan", "sienna","antiquewhite4","seashell4")


colors_p <- c("#065535", "darkseagreen4", "#3333CC", "#660033", "#FF9966", "#CC6600", "#CBD588", "#5F7FC7", "orange", "#DA5724", "#508578")

color_palette5 <- c("darkgreen","darkred","tan","#DBCA69","#D57500", "#613318", "#FF9966", "#CC6600", "#CBD588", "#5F7FC7", "orange", "#DA5724","antiquewhite4")

olivia_palette<-c("#d4a373","#a8dadc","#457b9d","#1d3557","#e63946")
```
#Setting a theme 
```{r}
mytheme <-   theme(panel.grid.minor=element_blank(), #gets rid of grey and lines in the middle
                   panel.grid.major=element_blank(), #gets rid of grey and lines in the middle
                   panel.background=element_rect(fill="white"),#gets rid of grey and lines in the middle
                   axis.title.x = element_text( size=14, color="black", face="bold"), #size of x-axis title
                   axis.title.y = element_text(size=14, color="black", face="bold"), #size of y-axis title
                   axis.text.x = element_text(size=12, color="black"),#size of x-axis text
                   axis.line=element_line(linewidth=1,color="black"),
                   axis.text.y = element_text(size=12, color="black"))#size of y-axis text
```

#Data Pre-Processing
##Loading in the data
```{r}
metadata <- read.delim("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/metadata_files/2023_SlovakiaRomania_DeepSoileDNA_metadatacsv.csv", sep = ",", row.names = 1)

taxonomy_data <- read.csv("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/metadata_files/Fungi_BLAST_Table.csv", sep = ",", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, header = TRUE)

otutab_fun  <-  read.delim("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/metadata_files/Fungi_OTU_Table.csv", sep = ",", row.names = 1)
```
##Taxonomy Best hits for fungi- tijana
```{r}
taxonomy_data_fun <- taxonomy_data

colnames(taxonomy_data_fun)

# Check for rows with STATUS == "NO HIT" 
taxonomy_data_fun <- taxonomy_data_fun %>%
  filter(STATUS != "NO HIT")

# Split SEQ TITLE into OTU_ID, Mostabund, and Size
taxonomy_data_fun <- taxonomy_data_fun %>%
  separate(`SEQ.TITLE`, into = c("OTU_ID", "Mostabund", "Size"), sep = "\\|", remove = TRUE, fill = "right")

# Split Description into taxonomic levels and other metadata, handle missing pieces
#taxonomy_data_fun <- taxonomy_data_fun %>%
  #separate(Description, into = c("Species_hypothesis", "Accession_number", "Taxonomy", "Ecology"), sep = "\\|", remove = TRUE, fill = "right") %>%
  #separate(Taxonomy, into = c("Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"), sep = ";", remove = TRUE, fill = "right") %>%
  #separate(Ecology, into = c("Ecology_primary"), sep = "\\*", extra = "drop", fill = "right")

#ALREADY DID THIS AS PART OF SEED2 TUTORIAL IN EXCEL.
colnames(taxonomy_data_fun)

#I'm a little confused on this step:
best_hits_fun <- taxonomy_data_fun %>%
  group_by(OTU_ID) %>%
  arrange(`E.value`) %>% # Ensure hits are sorted by E value within each OTU
  filter(!grepl("Incertae_sedis", Genus)) %>% # Exclude "Incertae_sedis" from Genus
  slice_head(n = 1) %>% # Select the first hit (best valid hit)
  ungroup()

# Step 2: Handle OTUs where all hits have "Incertae_sedis" in Genus
# For such cases, fall back to selecting the hit with the lowest E value
fallback_hits_fun <- taxonomy_data_fun %>%
  group_by(OTU_ID) %>%
  filter(grepl("Incertae_sedis", Genus)) %>% # Only consider "Incertae_sedis" hits
  slice_min(order_by = `E.value`, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>% # Select lowest E value
  ungroup()

# Combine the best hits with fallback hits where necessary
final_hits_fun <- bind_rows(best_hits_fun, fallback_hits_fun) %>%
  distinct(OTU_ID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>% # Ensure only one hit per OTU
  arrange(OTU_ID) # Sort by OTU_ID for clarity

# Add the "Genus_corrected" column if similarity is below 90%
final_hits_fun <- final_hits_fun %>%
  mutate(
    Genus_corrected = ifelse(`Similarity...` < 90, "unassigned", Genus),
    Ecology_corrected = ifelse(`Similarity...` < 90, "Unknown", FunctionalGuild1)
  )

# Print the resulting data frame to verify
```
##Combining data- tijana
```{r}
#Count how many sequences per OTU
otutab_fun$Counts <- rowSums(otutab_fun)
# Check the minimum number
min(otutab_fun$Counts) #when I run this, I get [1] 0 instead of [1] 1
otutab_fun <- otutab_fun[otutab_fun$Counts > 0, ]
min(otutab_fun$Counts) #this fixes it

#joining otu table with taxonomy
otutax_fun <- left_join(rownames_to_column(otutab_fun, "OTU_ID"), taxonomy_data_fun, by = "OTU_ID")

# Display fungal ecology categories 

unique(otutax_fun$FunctionalGuild1)

#getting rid of unnecessary columns- not in tijana's code
otutax_fun <- otutax_fun %>%
  select(-no.hit)

#displaying all columns in new joined table
colnames(otutax_fun)
# Singletons and non-fungal OTUs removed (sometimes there is non specific amplification of other eukaryots)
# Check if there is only fungi
unique(otutax_fun$Kingdom)

# Ecology categories are already chosen, we will remove prefixes from taxonomy
otutax_filtered_fun <- otutax_fun %>% 
  mutate(Genus = str_replace(Genus, "g__", ""),
         Phylum = str_replace(Phylum, "p__", ""),
         Kingdom = str_replace(Kingdom, "k__", "")) %>%
  filter(Counts > 1 & Kingdom == "Fungi" & E.value < 1e-50) %>%
  mutate(Genus = ifelse(Similarity... < 90, "unclassified", Genus),
         Family = ifelse(Similarity... < 85, "unclassified", Family),
         Order = ifelse(Similarity... < 80, "unclassified", Order),
         FunctionalGuild1 = ifelse(Similarity... < 90, "Unknown", FunctionalGuild1),
         Ecology_new = case_when(
           FunctionalGuild1 == "soil_saprotroph" ~ "Saprotroph (soil)",
      FunctionalGuild1 %in% c(
        "litter_saprotroph",
        "unspecified_saprotroph",
        "dung_saprotroph",
        "pollen_saprotroph"
      ) ~ "Saprotroph (other)",
      FunctionalGuild1 == "wood_saprotroph" ~ "Saprotroph (wood)",
      FunctionalGuild1 == "plant_pathogen" ~ "Plant pathogen",
      FunctionalGuild1 == "ectomycorrhizal" ~ "EcM",
      FunctionalGuild1 == "ericoid_mycorrhizal" ~ "ErM",
      FunctionalGuild1 == "Unknown" ~ "Unknown", 
      FunctionalGuild1 == "ND" ~ "Unknown" , # keep Unknown as Unknown
      TRUE ~ "Others"
         ))
# Check
unique(otutax_filtered_fun$Kingdom)

unique(otutax_filtered_fun$Ecology_new)

# Add Genus_Phylum column and Genus_ecology
otutax_filtered_fun$Genus_phylum <- paste0(otutax_filtered_fun$Genus, " (", otutax_filtered_fun$Phylum, ")")
head(unique(otutax_filtered_fun$Genus_phylum)) 

otutax_filtered_fun$Genus_ecology <- paste0(otutax_filtered_fun$Genus, " (", otutax_filtered_fun$Ecology_new, ")")
head(otutax_filtered_fun$Genus_ecology)

#split again into filtered otu and tax (to use in phyloseq) [, 2:(ncol(otu_table) + 1)]

otutab_filtered_fun <- otutax_filtered_fun %>% 
  select(1:161) %>% #takes out counts column
  remove_rownames() %>% 
  column_to_rownames(var = "OTU_ID")

tax_filtered_fun <- otutax_filtered_fun %>% 
  select(OTU_ID, Kingdom:Ecology_new, Genus_phylum:Genus_ecology) %>% 
  remove_rownames %>% 
  column_to_rownames(var = "OTU_ID")

```

#Alpha Diversity
##Preparing the data- Tijana
```{r}
# I prefer this way, but it can be done with phyloseq object as well

min_depth <- min(colSums(otutax_filtered_fun[,2:(ncol(otutab_filtered_fun) + 1)])) 
min_depth

# rarefying to the min number of sequences, this is important to have the samples comparable

t_otutable <- as.data.frame((t(otutab_filtered_fun))) #transpose otu table for use in vegan, rows are samples, columns are otus

# Using rrarefy from vegan -   random subsampling to same number of sequences
t_otus_rarefied <- as.data.frame(round(rrarefy(t_otutable, 4654)))

# Calculate richness, Shannon, evenness
# Richness = how many species are present?
# Shannon = How many species AND how evenly distributed are they?
# Evenness = are species equally abundant, regardless of how many there are?

richness <- as.data.frame(specnumber(t_otus_rarefied, MARGIN = 1)) %>% setnames("richness") 
shannon <- as.data.frame(diversity(t_otus_rarefied, index = "shannon", MARGIN = 1, base = exp(1))) %>% setnames("shannon") 
evenness <- as.data.frame(shannon/log(richness)) %>% setnames("evenness")

# Combine diversity into one table

diversity <- print(list(rownames_to_column(metadata, "Sample_ID"), 
                       rownames_to_column(richness, "Sample_ID"), 
                       rownames_to_column(shannon, "Sample_ID"), 
                       rownames_to_column(evenness, "Sample_ID"))) %>%
  reduce(left_join, by = "Sample_ID")

write.csv(diversity, "C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/metadata_files/diversity.csv")

#the low diversity associated with HOF120 corresponds to the incredibly low read count (4)- check notepad file 

colnames(diversity)

diversity_summary <-  diversity  %>%
  group_by(Country, Forest_Stand, Soil_Horizon) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean_richness= mean(richness),
            sd_richness = sd(richness),
            n_richness = n(), 
            SE_richness = sd(richness)/sqrt(n()),
            mean_shannon= mean(shannon),
            sd_shannon = sd(shannon),
            n_shannon = n(), 
            SE_shannon = sd(shannon)/sqrt(n()),
            mean_evenness= mean(evenness),
            sd_evenness = sd(evenness),
            n_evenness = n(), 
            SE_evenness = sd(evenness)/sqrt(n()))

diversity_summary
```
##Plotting Alpha Diversity- tijana
```{r}
# Create ymin and ymax columns for richness, shannon, and evennes

diversity_summary <- diversity_summary %>%
  mutate(ymin_richness = mean_richness - sd_richness,
         ymax_richness = mean_richness + sd_richness,
         ymin_shannon = mean_shannon - sd_shannon,
         ymax_shannon = mean_shannon + sd_shannon,
         ymin_evenness = mean_evenness - sd_evenness,
         ymax_evenness = mean_evenness + sd_evenness)

diversity_summary$Soil_Horizon <- factor (diversity_summary$Soil_Horizon, 
                                                      levels = c("FH",
                                                                 "1",
                                                                 "2", "3", "4"))


# The plot, using ggplot package
# This is a function to create our plots, it can be customized

generate_diversity_plot <- function(data, y_var, y_label, ymin, ymax, fill_var, color_palette) {
  ggplot(data, aes(x = Soil_Horizon, y = !!sym(y_var), fill = !!sym(fill_var))) + 
    geom_col(color = "black") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = !!sym(ymin), ymax = !!sym(ymax)), width = 0.2) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
          strip.text.x = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 8), 
          legend.title = element_blank(),
          legend.position = "bottom") + 
    guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 3)) +
    labs(y = y_label) +
    facet_grid(~Country+Forest_Stand)
}


# Generate plots using previously created fungion


plot_richness <- generate_diversity_plot(diversity_summary, "mean_richness", "Mean Richness", "ymin_richness", "ymax_richness", "Soil_Horizon", color_palette5)
plot_shannon <- generate_diversity_plot(diversity_summary, "mean_shannon", "Mean Shannon", "ymin_shannon", "ymax_shannon", "Soil_Horizon", color_palette5)
plot_evenness <- generate_diversity_plot(diversity_summary, "mean_evenness", "Mean Evenness", "ymin_evenness", "ymax_evenness", "Soil_Horizon", color_palette5)

# Combine three graphs into one with ggarrange

arranged_fun_div <- ggarrange(plot_richness, plot_shannon, plot_evenness, nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE)

# Annotate the arranged plots with a custom title at the bottom
annotated_fun_div <- annotate_figure(arranged_fun_div,
                                  top = text_grob("Alpha diversity by depth", 
                                                     color = "black", size = 14, 
                                                     face = "bold"))

# Plot combined and annotated graph
plot(annotated_fun_div)
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/alphadiversity_tijana/annotated_fun_div.png",plot=annotated_fun_div,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")

```
##Stats for Alpha Diversity- tijana
###ANOVA- tijana
```{r}
#NOTE: I did more in depth stuff with my nlme models below for continuous (y) against continuous (x)
# Here is the base code of using ANOVA (aov funcion) in vegan package 

#ANOVA for continuous variables (y) against categorical variables (x)

# Is richness significantly different between roots, soil, and litter?

summary(aov(richness ~ Soil_Horizon, data = diversity))
# yes

# How about forest management?

summary(aov(richness ~ Forest_Stand, data = diversity))
# no

# Here is the code to make a function that does anova automatically for selected list of predictors, and then run it for richness, shannon, evenness

# Perform ANOVA
perform_anova <- function(response, predictor, data) {
  aov_result <- aov(reformulate(predictor, response), data = data)
  summary_df <- broom::tidy(aov_result)
  summary_df$response <- response
  summary_df$predictor <- predictor
  return(summary_df)
}

# List of predictors
predictors <- c("Soil_Horizon", "Country","Forest_Stand") #these are all categorical variables

# Perform ANOVA for richness, shannon, and evenness, and combine results
anova_results <- bind_rows(
  lapply(predictors, perform_anova, response = "richness", data = diversity),
  lapply(predictors, perform_anova, response = "shannon", data = diversity),
  lapply(predictors, perform_anova, response = "evenness", data = diversity)
)

# Display the combined summary in a table using kable
anova_results %>%
  select(response, predictor, term, df, sumsq, meansq, statistic, p.value) %>%
  kable(format = "html", caption = "ANOVA Summary for Richness, Shannon, and Evenness") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"))
```

##Tukey's test- tijana 
```{r}
# Function to perform Tukey's HSD test and extract results
perform_tukey <- function(response, predictor, data) {
  model <- aov(reformulate(predictor, response), data = data)
  tukey <- HSD.test(model, predictor, group = TRUE)
  tukey_df <- as.data.frame(tukey$groups)
  tukey_df$Treatment <- rownames(tukey_df)
  tukey_df$response <- response
  tukey_df$predictor <- predictor
  return(tukey_df)
}

# Function to calculate means for each level of a factor
calculate_means <- function(response, predictor, data) {
  mean_df <- data %>%
    group_by(!!sym(predictor)) %>%
    summarise(mean_value = mean(!!sym(response), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    mutate(Treatment = !!sym(predictor)) %>%
    select(Treatment, mean_value)
  mean_df$response <- response
  mean_df$predictor <- predictor
  return(mean_df)
}

# Perform Tukey's test and calculate means for each combination of predictor and response
tukey_results <- bind_rows(
  lapply(predictors, function(p) perform_tukey("richness", p, data = diversity)),
  lapply(predictors, function(p) perform_tukey("shannon", p, data = diversity)),
  lapply(predictors, function(p) perform_tukey("evenness", p, data = diversity))
)


# Display Tukey's test results with means in a table using kable

tukey_results %>%
  select(response, predictor, Treatment, groups) %>%
  kable(format = "html", caption = "Tukey's Test Results for Richness, Shannon, and Evenness") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"))

#from Tijana's code: We see that compartments are all different from each other when it comes to richness, but for shannon Litter and Soil are similar (both contain a), Soil and Roots are similar (both contain b), and Litter and Roots are different (a vs b)
# Assuming 'diversity_summary' is your final combined data frame
knitr::kable(diversity_summary, 
             caption = "Combined Summary for Richness, Shannon, and Evenness")
```
##NLME for richness against environmental factors (regression)-olivia
###Preparing the data
```{r}
# ----------------------------
# LOADING MY PACKAGES AND PLOTS
# ----------------------------
diversity #this is where all my data is
diversity$Soil_Horizon <- factor(diversity$Soil_Horizon,
                               levels = c("FH", "1", "2", "3", "4"))
diversity$Plot_ID <- as.factor(diversity$Plot_ID)

library(GGally)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(nlme)
library(car)

table(diversity$Soil_Horizon, diversity$Plot_ID)
# or more simply:
diversity %>%
  group_by(Soil_Horizon) %>%
  summarise(n_obs = n(), n_plots = n_distinct(Plot_ID))

# ----------------------------
# CHECKING FOR CORRELATIONS 
# ----------------------------
ggpairsplot<- ggpairs(
  diversity %>%
    select(Soil_Horizon,
           C_in_g_per_m_square,
           N_in_g_per_m_square,
           CN_ratio_g_per_m_square,
           richness,
           shannon,
           evenness),
  upper = list(continuous = wrap("cor", method= "pearson",size = 3.5, stars = TRUE)),
  lower = list(continuous = wrap("points", alpha = 0.6, size = 1)),
  diag  = list(continuous = "densityDiag")
)
ggpairsplot

# ANOVA — tests if continuous variable differs across categorical groups
summary(aov(C_in_g_per_m_square ~ Soil_Horizon, data = diversity))


ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/alphadiversity_nlme/ggpairsplot.png",plot=ggpairsplot,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")
#CORRELATIONS
#*** 0.001 
#** 0.01
#* 0.05
#. 0.10

# ----------------------------
# CHECKING FOR NORMALITY OF DATASET
# ----------------------------

shapiro.test(diversity$richness) #while this is important, this can be validated later with qqplots of residuals of model
#evenness: not normal
#shannon: not normal
#richness: 0.02: not normal

# ----------------------------
# OPTIONAL SCALING OF VARIABLES (?)
# ----------------------------

#diversity_scaled <- diversity %>%
  #mutate(across(
    #c(Soil_depth_horizon_in_cm,
      #C_in_g_per_m_square,
      #N_in_g_per_m_square,
      #CN_ratio_g_per_m_square),
    #scale
 # ))
```
###How is C and N (continuous) influenced by soil depth (categorical)? 
```{r}
# ----------------------------
# How is soil carbon influenced by depth?
# ----------------------------

carbondepthmodel<-lme(C_in_g_per_m_square ~ Soil_Horizon, random=list(Plot_ID=~1), data = diversity)
summary(carbondepthmodel)
summary(carbondepthmodel)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(carbondepthmodel)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #are these normal residuals 
Anova(carbondepthmodel)

#RESULTS
#carbon peaks in specific non-FH horizons, but overall increases as we go down- this aligns with Laxmi's paper. 
#carbon is lowest in the FH layer- look at the "value" in the summary table
#Soil horizon is an extremely significant predictor of carbon overall (p<0.001)

# ----------------------------
# How is soil nitrogen influenced by depth? 
# ----------------------------
#make sure soil horizon is still a factor, here's how to check:
table(diversity$Soil_Horizon)
levels(diversity$Soil_Horizon)

nitrogendepthmodel<-lme(N_in_g_per_m_square ~ Soil_Horizon, random=list(Plot_ID=~1), data = diversity)
summary(nitrogendepthmodel)
summary(nitrogendepthmodel)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(nitrogendepthmodel)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #are these normal residuals?
Anova(nitrogendepthmodel)

#RESULTS
#these are essentially the results of laxmi's paper

```
###How is fungal richness (continuous) influenced by environmental factors? 
```{r}

# ----------------------------
# How is fungal richness/shannon/evenness influenced by soil depth and carbon?
# ----------------------------

#richness
crichnessmodel<-lme(richness ~ C_in_g_per_m_square +Soil_Horizon, random=list(Plot_ID=~1), data = diversity) 
summary(crichnessmodel)
summary(crichnessmodel)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(crichnessmodel)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal residuals
# Histogram of residuals
Anova(crichnessmodel) #get p values and chi square from here

#RESULTS
#after accounting for soil horizon, carbon is not a significant predictor of richness. soil horizon is significantly decreasing fungal richness as you go deeper in the profile. 

#shannon 
crichnessmodel1<-lme(shannon ~ C_in_g_per_m_square +Soil_Horizon, random=list(Plot_ID=~1), data = diversity) 
summary(crichnessmodel1)
summary(crichnessmodel1)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(crichnessmodel1)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal residuals
# Histogram of residuals
Anova(crichnessmodel1)

#evenness
crichnessmodel2<-lme(evenness ~ C_in_g_per_m_square +Soil_Horizon, random=list(Plot_ID=~1), data = diversity) 
summary(crichnessmodel2)
summary(crichnessmodel2)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(crichnessmodel2)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal residuals
# Histogram of residuals
Anova(crichnessmodel2)


# ----------------------------
# How is fungal richness influenced by soil depth and nitrogen?
# ----------------------------
Nrichnessmodel<-lme(richness ~ N_in_g_per_m_square +Soil_Horizon, random=list(Plot_ID=~1), data = diversity) 
summary(Nrichnessmodel)
summary(Nrichnessmodel)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(Nrichnessmodel)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal residuals
# Histogram of residuals
Anova(Nrichnessmodel)
#RESULTS
#after accounting for soil horizon, nitrogen is not a significant predictor or richness. soil horizon is significantly decreasing fungal richness as you go deeper in the profile. 

# ----------------------------
# How is fungal richness influenced by soil depth and C:N ratio?
# ----------------------------
CNrichnessmodel<-lme(richness ~ CN_ratio_g_per_m_square +Soil_Horizon, random=list(Plot_ID=~1), data = diversity) 
summary(CNrichnessmodel)
summary(CNrichnessmodel)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(CNrichnessmodel)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal residuals
# Histogram of residuals
Anova(CNrichnessmodel)
#RESULTS
#after accounting for soil horizon, C:N ratio is  a significant predictor of richness. soil horizon is significantly decreasing fungal richness as you go deeper in the profile. 

#checking r2 at the end because theres issues with the mumin package 
#reminder: conditional r squared is amount of explained variance for the entire model; marginal r squared: how much of this variance is attributed to the fixed effects alone
library(MuMIn)
r.squaredGLMM(carbondepthmodel) 
r.squaredGLMM(nitrogendepthmodel) 
r.squaredGLMM(crichnessmodel) 
r.squaredGLMM(Nrichnessmodel) 
r.squaredGLMM(CNrichnessmodel) 

```
###Trying the nlme models per soil layer
```{r}
#PER SOIL HORIZON LAYER *WIP
#BEFORE RUNNING THESE NEXT MODELS: might think about log transforming the species richness- the qqplots for some of them are a bit concerning. 

# ----------------------------
# FH LAYER: how is fungal richness influenced by carbon?
# ----------------------------

FH_data <- subset(diversity, Soil_Horizon == "FH")

FH_model <- lme(richness ~ C_in_g_per_m_square,
                random=list(Plot_ID=~1),
                data = FH_data)
summary(FH_model)
summary(FH_model)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(FH_model)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal residuals
r.squaredGLMM(FH_model) 
Anova(FH_model) #carbon is a significant predictor (0.03)- this is interesting because it's the only layer where it is

# ----------------------------
# 1 LAYER: how is fungal richness influenced by carbon?
# ----------------------------

sub1_data <- subset(diversity, Soil_Horizon == "1")

sub1_model <- lme(richness ~ C_in_g_per_m_square,
                random=list(Plot_ID=~1),
                data = sub1_data)
summary(sub1_model)
summary(sub1_model)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(sub1_model)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal (ish?) residuals
library(performance)
r2_nakagawa(sub1_model)
Anova(sub1_model) #carbon is not really a significant predictor in this layer (0.059)

# ----------------------------
# 2 LAYER: how is fungal richness influenced by carbon?
# ----------------------------

sub2_data <- subset(diversity, Soil_Horizon == "2")

sub2_model <- lme(richness ~ C_in_g_per_m_square,
                random=list(Plot_ID=~1),
                data = sub2_data)
summary(sub2_model)
summary(sub2_model)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(sub2_model)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal (ish?) residuals- maybe we should log transform these 
library(performance)
r2_nakagawa(sub2_model)
Anova(sub2_model) #carbon is not a significant predictor in this layer (0.82)


# ----------------------------
# 3 LAYER: how is fungal richness influenced by carbon?
# ----------------------------

sub3_data <- subset(diversity, Soil_Horizon == "3")

sub3_model <- lme(richness ~ C_in_g_per_m_square,
                random=list(Plot_ID=~1),
                data = sub3_data)
summary(sub3_model)
summary(sub3_model)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(sub3_model)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal (ish?) residuals
library(performance)
r2_nakagawa(sub3_model)
Anova(sub3_model) #carbon is not a significant predictor in this layer

# ----------------------------
# 4 LAYER: how is fungal richness influenced by carbon?
# ----------------------------

sub4_data <- subset(diversity, Soil_Horizon == "4")

sub4_model <- lme(richness ~ C_in_g_per_m_square,
                random=list(Plot_ID=~1),
                data = sub4_data)
summary(sub4_model)
summary(sub4_model)$tTable
residuals<-residuals(sub4_model)
qqnorm(residuals)
qqline(residuals,col="red") #normal (ish?) residuals
library(performance)
r2_nakagawa(sub4_model)
Anova(sub4_model) #carbon is not a significant predictor in this layer

#so, carbon is only a significant predictor of fungal species richness in the FH layer.

```

##Plotting things together; creating predictive regression models 
```{r}

library(sjPlot)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggeffects)
library(cowplot)

# ---------------------------------------
# Carbon by depth
# ---------------------------------------
plot_A <- predict_response(
  carbondepthmodel,
  terms = "Soil_Horizon",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_A <- ggplot(plot_A, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
                width = 0.2) +
  labs(x = "Soil horizon",
       y = "Belowground carbon",
       title = "Soil horizon effect on carbon") +
  theme_bw()

plot_A
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/plot_A.png",plot=plot_A,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")

# ---------------------------------------
# nitrogen by depth *WIP
# ---------------------------------------


# =========================================================
# 1. PLOTTING CARBON EFFECT ON RICHNESS (continuous) AND SOIL HORIZON (CATEGORICAL) ON RICHNESS
# =========================================================
#richness VS CARBON
plot_C_richness <- predict_response(
  crichnessmodel,
  terms = "C_in_g_per_m_square",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_C_richness <- ggplot(plot_C_richness, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "Soil carbon",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Carbon effect") +
  theme_bw()

plot_C_richness

#richness vs soil horizon

plot_Horizon_richness <- predict_response(
  crichnessmodel, #this is in C model
  terms = "Soil_Horizon",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_Horizon_richness <- ggplot(plot_Horizon_richness, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
                width = 0.2) +
  labs(x = "Soil horizon",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Soil horizon effect") +
  theme_bw()

plot_Horizon_richness

C_horizon_richness_plot <- plot_grid(plot_C_richness, plot_Horizon_richness, ncol = 2)

C_horizon_richness_plot

ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/alphadiversity_nlme/C_horizon_richness_plot.png",plot=C_horizon_richness_plot,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")

# =========================================================
# 2. PLOTTING NITROGEN EFFECT ON RICHNESS (continuous) AND SOIL HORIZON (CATEGORICAL) ON RICHNESS
# =========================================================
#richness VS NITROGEN
plot_N_richness <- predict_response(
  Nrichnessmodel,
  terms = "N_in_g_per_m_square",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_N_richness <- ggplot(plot_N_richness, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "Soil Nitrogen",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Nitrogen effect") +
  theme_bw()

plot_N_richness

#richness vs soil horizon

plot_Horizon_richness_N <- predict_response(
  Nrichnessmodel, #this is in n model
  terms = "Soil_Horizon",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_Horizon_richness_N <- ggplot(plot_Horizon_richness_N, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
                width = 0.2) +
  labs(x = "Soil horizon",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Soil horizon effect") +
  theme_bw()

plot_Horizon_richness_N

N_horizon_richness_plot <- plot_grid(plot_N_richness, plot_Horizon_richness_N, ncol = 2)

N_horizon_richness_plot

ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/alphadiversity_nlme/N_horizon_richness_plot.png",plot=N_horizon_richness_plot,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")

# =========================================================
# 3. PLOTTING C:N RATIO EFFECT ON RICHNESS (continuous) AND SOIL HORIZON (CATEGORICAL) ON RICHNESS
# =========================================================
#richness VS c:N ratio
plot_CN_richness <- predict_response(
  CNrichnessmodel,
  terms = "CN_ratio_g_per_m_square",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_CN_richness <- ggplot(plot_CN_richness, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "Soil C:N Ratio",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "C:N ratio effect") +
  theme_bw()

plot_CN_richness

#richness vs soil horizon

plot_Horizon_richness_CN <- predict_response(
  CNrichnessmodel, #this is in CN model
  terms = "Soil_Horizon",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_Horizon_richness_CN <- ggplot(plot_Horizon_richness_CN, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
                width = 0.2) +
  labs(x = "Soil horizon",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Soil horizon effect") +
  theme_bw()

plot_Horizon_richness_CN

CN_horizon_richness_plot <- plot_grid(plot_CN_richness, plot_Horizon_richness_CN, ncol = 2)

CN_horizon_richness_plot

ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/alphadiversity_nlme/CN_horizon_richness_plot.png",plot=CN_horizon_richness_plot,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")


###PER LAYER###

plot_FH <- predict_response(
  FH_model,
  terms = "C_in_g_per_m_square",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_FH <- ggplot(plot_FH, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "Soil carbon",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Carbon effect in FH layer") +
  theme_bw()

plot_1 <- predict_response(
  sub1_model,
  terms = "C_in_g_per_m_square",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_1 <- ggplot(plot_1, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "Soil carbon",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Carbon effect in 1 layer") +
  theme_bw()

plot_2 <- predict_response(
  sub2_model,
  terms = "C_in_g_per_m_square",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_2 <- ggplot(plot_2, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "Soil carbon",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Carbon effect in 2 layer") +
  theme_bw()

plot_3 <- predict_response(
  sub3_model,
  terms = "C_in_g_per_m_square",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_3 <- ggplot(plot_3, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "Soil carbon",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Carbon effect in 3 layer") +
  theme_bw()

plot_4 <- predict_response(
  sub4_model,
  terms = "C_in_g_per_m_square",
  ci_level = 0.95
)

plot_4 <- ggplot(plot_4, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "Soil carbon",
       y = "Predicted richness",
       title = "Carbon effect in 4 layer") +
  theme_bw()

layerplots <- plot_grid(plot_FH, plot_1, plot_2, plot_3, plot_4, ncol = 5)

layerplots
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/layerplots.png",plot=layerplots,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")

```
#Beta Diversity
##Plotting NMDS
###DONT USE Plotting exploratory NMDS for soil horizon vs forest stand (no environmental arrows)- tijana
```{r}

library(vegan)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ----------------------------
# 1. COMMUNITY MATRIX
# ----------------------------

otu_hell_fun <- as.data.frame(decostand(t_otutable, method = "hell"))

# remove unwanted sample
otu_hell_fun <- otu_hell_fun[rownames(otu_hell_fun) != "HOF120", ]

# ----------------------------
# 2. METADATA
# ----------------------------

metadata_clean <- metadata
metadata_clean <- metadata_clean[rownames(metadata_clean) != "HOF120", ]

mapping2 <- data.frame(Sample_names = rownames(metadata_clean),
                       metadata_clean,
                       row.names = NULL)

# align order to community matrix
mapping2 <- mapping2[match(rownames(otu_hell_fun), mapping2$Sample_names), ]

# safety check
stopifnot(all(rownames(otu_hell_fun) == mapping2$Sample_names))

# ----------------------------
# 3. NMDS
# ----------------------------

set.seed(123)
veganNMDS <- metaMDS(otu_hell_fun, distance = "bray")

# extract NMDS scores (NO tibble)
nmds_points <- as.data.frame(veganNMDS$points)
nmds_points$Sample_names <- rownames(nmds_points)

# rename axes
colnames(nmds_points)[1:2] <- c("NMDS1", "NMDS2")

# merge with metadata
final_NMDS <- merge(nmds_points, mapping2, by = "Sample_names")

# ----------------------------
# 4. NMDS PLOT (NO ENVFIT)
# ----------------------------

NMDS_plot <- ggplot(final_NMDS,
                    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2,
                        color = Forest_Stand,
                        shape = Soil_Horizon)) +

  geom_point(size = 6) +

  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position = "left") +

  labs(title = "NMDS of microbial communities") +

  scale_color_manual(values = color_palette5)

NMDS_plot

ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/NMDS_plot_only.png",plot=NMDS_plot,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")
```
###NMDS with convex hulls (organized by soil horizon)- olivia/tijana
```{r}
# ----------------------------
# NMDS WITH CONVEX HULLS
# ----------------------------

library(vegan)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ----------------------------
# 1. COMMUNITY MATRIX
# ----------------------------
otu_hell_fun <- as.data.frame(decostand(t_otutable, method = "hell"))
otu_hell_fun <- otu_hell_fun[rownames(otu_hell_fun) != "HOF120", ]

# ----------------------------
# 2. METADATA
# ----------------------------
metadata_clean <- metadata
metadata_clean <- metadata_clean[rownames(metadata_clean) != "HOF120", ]
mapping2 <- data.frame(Sample_names = rownames(metadata_clean),
                       metadata_clean,
                       row.names = NULL)
mapping2 <- mapping2[match(rownames(otu_hell_fun), mapping2$Sample_names), ]
stopifnot(all(rownames(otu_hell_fun) == mapping2$Sample_names))

# ----------------------------
# 3. NMDS
# ----------------------------
set.seed(123)
veganNMDS <- metaMDS(otu_hell_fun, distance = "bray")
nmds_points <- as.data.frame(veganNMDS$points)
nmds_points$Sample_names <- rownames(nmds_points)
colnames(nmds_points)[1:2] <- c("NMDS1", "NMDS2")
final_NMDS <- merge(nmds_points, mapping2, by = "Sample_names")

# ensure correct factor level order
final_NMDS$Soil_Horizon <- factor(final_NMDS$Soil_Horizon,
                                   levels = c("FH", "1", "2", "3", "4"))

# ----------------------------
# 4. CONVEX HULLS
# ----------------------------
hull_data <- final_NMDS %>%
  group_by(Soil_Horizon) %>%
  slice(chull(NMDS1, NMDS2))

# ----------------------------
# 5. PLOT
# ----------------------------
NMDS_plot_hulls <- ggplot(final_NMDS,
                          aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
  geom_polygon(data = hull_data,
               aes(fill = Soil_Horizon,
                   color = Soil_Horizon),
               alpha = 0.15,
               linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Soil_Horizon),
             size = 4) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position = "left") +
  labs(title = "NMDS of fungal communities across forest stands") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FH"  = "#d4a373",
                                 "1"   = "#a8dadc",
                                 "2"   = "#457b9d",
                                 "3"   = "#1d3557",
                                 "4"   = "#e63946")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("FH"  = "#d4a373",
                                "1"   = "#a8dadc",
                                "2"   = "#457b9d",
                                "3"   = "#1d3557",
                                "4"   = "#e63946"))

NMDS_plot_hulls
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/soilhorizon/NMDS_plot_hulls_soilhorizon.png",plot=NMDS_plot_hulls,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")
```

###Plotting NMDS for soil horizon vs forest stand with environmental arrows
```{r}
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
#adapted from tijana's code 

# ----------------------------
# ENVFIT
#here's where i would add other environmental variables that are continuous (e.g. soil ph)
# ----------------------------

set.seed(674375397)

data.envfit.nmds <- envfit(
  veganNMDS,
  mapping2[, c("C_in_g_per_m_square",
               "N_in_g_per_m_square",
               "CN_ratio_g_per_m_square",
               "Total_C_in_Mg_per_ha")]
)

# extract vectors
data.envfit.spec.nmds <- as.data.frame(
  scores(data.envfit.nmds, display = "vectors") *
    ordiArrowMul(data.envfit.nmds)
)

data.envfit.plot.nmds <- data.envfit.spec.nmds
data.envfit.plot.nmds$variables <- rownames(data.envfit.spec.nmds)

# add p-values
data.envfit.plot.nmds$pval <- data.envfit.nmds$vectors$pvals[
  match(data.envfit.plot.nmds$variables,
        names(data.envfit.nmds$vectors$pvals))
]

# scale arrows
data.envfit.plot.nmds[,1:2] <- data.envfit.plot.nmds[,1:2] * 3

# ----------------------------
# PLOT WITH ENVFIT
# ----------------------------

data.envfit.plot.nmds <- data.envfit.plot.nmds %>%
  mutate(variables = case_when(
    variables=="C_in_g_per_m_square"~"Belowground_Carbon",
    variables=="N_in_g_per_m_square"~"Belowground_Nitrogen",
    variables=="CN_ratio_g_per_m_square"~"Belowground_CN_ratio",
    variables=="Total_C_in_Mg_per_ha"~"Aboveground_Carbon",
    TRUE~variables
  ))


NMDS_plot_envfit <- ggplot(final_NMDS,
                           aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2,
                               color = Soil_Horizon)) +

  geom_point(size = 2) +

  geom_segment(data = data.envfit.plot.nmds,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0,
                   xend = NMDS1, yend = NMDS2,
                   linetype = pval < 0.05),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm")),
               color = "black",
               linewidth = 1,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +

  geom_text(data = data.envfit.plot.nmds,
            aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, label = variables),
            size = 2,
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +

  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position = "left") +

  scale_color_manual(values = olivia_palette) +

 scale_linetype_manual(name = "Significance",
                        values = c("TRUE" = "dashed", "FALSE" = "solid"),
                        labels = c("TRUE" = "p < 0.05", "FALSE" = "p ≥ 0.05")) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(order = 1),
         color = guide_legend(order = 2),
         linetype = guide_legend(order = 3))
NMDS_plot_envfit

ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/soilhorizon/NMDS_plot_envfit.png",plot=NMDS_plot_envfit,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")


data.envfit.nmds$vectors$pvals
#belowground c and n as well as CN ratio drive fungal richness to be different 

#beta diversity statistics came from tijana's code. 

```
###NMDS with convex hulls (organized by country)- olivia/tijana
```{r}
# ----------------------------
# NMDS WITH CONVEX HULLS
# ----------------------------

library(vegan)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ----------------------------
# 1. COMMUNITY MATRIX
# ----------------------------
otu_hell_fun <- as.data.frame(decostand(t_otutable, method = "hell"))
otu_hell_fun <- otu_hell_fun[rownames(otu_hell_fun) != "HOF120", ]

# ----------------------------
# 2. METADATA
# ----------------------------
metadata_clean <- metadata
metadata_clean <- metadata_clean[rownames(metadata_clean) != "HOF120", ]
mapping2 <- data.frame(Sample_names = rownames(metadata_clean),
                       metadata_clean,
                       row.names = NULL)
mapping2 <- mapping2[match(rownames(otu_hell_fun), mapping2$Sample_names), ]
stopifnot(all(rownames(otu_hell_fun) == mapping2$Sample_names))

# ----------------------------
# 3. NMDS
# ----------------------------
set.seed(123)
veganNMDS <- metaMDS(otu_hell_fun, distance = "bray")
nmds_points <- as.data.frame(veganNMDS$points)
nmds_points$Sample_names <- rownames(nmds_points)
colnames(nmds_points)[1:2] <- c("NMDS1", "NMDS2")
final_NMDS <- merge(nmds_points, mapping2, by = "Sample_names")

# ----------------------------
# 4. CONVEX HULLS
# ----------------------------
hull_data <- final_NMDS %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  slice(chull(NMDS1, NMDS2))

# ----------------------------
# 5. PLOT
# ----------------------------
NMDS_plot_hulls_country <- ggplot(final_NMDS,
                          aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
  geom_polygon(data = hull_data,
               aes(fill = Country,
                   color = Country),
               alpha = 0.15,
               linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Country),
             size = 4) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position = "left") +
  labs(title = "NMDS of fungal communities across countries") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Romania"  = "#d4a373",
                                 "Slovakia"   = "#a8dadc"))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Romania"  = "#d4a373",
                                "Slovakia"   = "#a8dadc"))

NMDS_plot_hulls_country
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/country/NMDS_plot_hulls_country.png",plot=NMDS_plot_hulls_country,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")

```
###NMDS with convex hulls (organized by forest stands)
```{r}
# ----------------------------
# NMDS WITH CONVEX HULLS
# ----------------------------

library(vegan)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ----------------------------
# 1. COMMUNITY MATRIX
# ----------------------------
otu_hell_fun <- as.data.frame(decostand(t_otutable, method = "hell"))
otu_hell_fun <- otu_hell_fun[rownames(otu_hell_fun) != "HOF120", ]

# ----------------------------
# 2. METADATA
# ----------------------------
metadata_clean <- metadata
metadata_clean <- metadata_clean[rownames(metadata_clean) != "HOF120", ]
mapping2 <- data.frame(Sample_names = rownames(metadata_clean),
                       metadata_clean,
                       row.names = NULL)
mapping2 <- mapping2[match(rownames(otu_hell_fun), mapping2$Sample_names), ]
stopifnot(all(rownames(otu_hell_fun) == mapping2$Sample_names))

# ----------------------------
# 3. NMDS
# ----------------------------
set.seed(123)
veganNMDS <- metaMDS(otu_hell_fun, distance = "bray")
nmds_points <- as.data.frame(veganNMDS$points)
nmds_points$Sample_names <- rownames(nmds_points)
colnames(nmds_points)[1:2] <- c("NMDS1", "NMDS2")
final_NMDS <- merge(nmds_points, mapping2, by = "Sample_names")

# 4. CONVEX HULLS
# ----------------------------
hull_data <- final_NMDS %>%
  group_by(Forest_Stand) %>%
  slice(chull(NMDS1, NMDS2))
# ----------------------------
# 5. PLOT
# ----------------------------
NMDS_plot_hulls1 <- ggplot(final_NMDS,
                          aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
  geom_polygon(data = hull_data,
               aes(fill = Forest_Stand,
                   color = Forest_Stand),
               alpha = 0.15,
               linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Forest_Stand),
             size = 4) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position = "left") +
  labs(title = "NMDS of fungal communities (by forest stand)") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Badin"  = "darkred",
                                 "Sinca"   = "darkgreen",
                                 "Dobroc"   = "tan",
                                 "Izvoarele_Nerei "   = "darkblue"))+
scale_fill_manual(values = c("Badin"  = "darkred",
                                 "Sinca"   = "darkgreen",
                                 "Dobroc"   = "tan",
                                 "Izvoarele_Nerei "   = "darkblue"))

NMDS_plot_hulls1
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/foreststand/NMDS_plot_hulls_foreststand.png",plot=NMDS_plot_hulls1,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")
```
##Stats for beta diversity
```{r}
# combining otu table and environmental data (good to combine to avoid mismatching), to be used for PERMANOVA
# We need our hellinger transformed OTU table, and the metadata+diversity 


all_permanova_fun <- left_join(rownames_to_column(otu_hell_fun, "Sample_ID"), 
                          diversity, by = "Sample_ID")

s.dis_fun <- vegan::vegdist(all_permanova_fun[, 2:(ncol(otutab_filtered_fun) + 1)]) # calculating Bray-Curtis dissimilarity (taking only OTU table from combined, 

```
###Beta Dispersion
```{r}
#SOIL HORIZONS#
# Check beta dispersion of the data, if there is high variability within treatments

beta_disp <- betadisper(s.dis_fun, all_permanova_fun$Soil_Horizon)
plot(beta_disp) #export this image

permutest(beta_disp, permutations = 999)

anova (beta_disp)

distances <- as.data.frame(beta_disp$distances)  # Convert distances to a data frame
groups <- as.factor(beta_disp$group)  # Make sure groups are factors

# Create a data frame for plotting
data_for_plot <- data.frame(Distance = distances[,1], Group = groups)

# Create the boxplot using ggplot2
betadisp_SH<-ggplot(data_for_plot, aes(x = Group, y = Distance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Beta Dispersion by Group",
       x = "Group",
       y = "Distance to Centroid") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, color = "blue", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.5)
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/soilhorizon/betadisp_SoilHorizon.png",plot=betadisp_SH,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")

#FOREST STANDS#
# Check beta dispersion of the data, if there is high variability within treatments

beta_disp1 <- betadisper(s.dis_fun, all_permanova_fun$Forest_Stand)
plot(beta_disp1) #export this image

permutest(beta_disp1, permutations = 999)

anova (beta_disp1)

distances <- as.data.frame(beta_disp1$distances)  # Convert distances to a data frame
groups <- as.factor(beta_disp1$group)  # Make sure groups are factors

# Create a data frame for plotting
data_for_plot1 <- data.frame(Distance = distances[,1], Group = groups)

# Create the boxplot using ggplot2
betadisp_ForestStand<-ggplot(data_for_plot1, aes(x = Group, y = Distance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Beta Dispersion by Group",
       x = "Group",
       y = "Distance to Centroid") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, color = "blue", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.5)
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/foreststand/betadisp_ForestStand.png",plot=betadisp_ForestStand,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")

#COUNTRIES#
# Check beta dispersion of the data, if there is high variability within treatments

beta_disp2 <- betadisper(s.dis_fun, all_permanova_fun$Country)
plot(beta_disp2) #export this image

permutest(beta_disp2, permutations = 999)

anova (beta_disp2)

distances <- as.data.frame(beta_disp2$distances)  # Convert distances to a data frame
groups <- as.factor(beta_disp2$group)  # Make sure groups are factors

# Create a data frame for plotting
data_for_plot2 <- data.frame(Distance = distances[,1], Group = groups)

# Create the boxplot using ggplot2
betadisp_country<-ggplot(data_for_plot2, aes(x = Group, y = Distance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Beta Dispersion by Group",
       x = "Group",
       y = "Distance to Centroid") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, color = "blue", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.5)
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/country/betadisp_country.png",plot=betadisp_country,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")

```
###Permanova Models
```{r}
# testing the difference in compartments (categorical variable of soil horizon)
adonis2(s.dis_fun~all_permanova_fun$Soil_Horizon, permutations = 999)
#(categorical variable of soil horizon and forest stand)
adonis2(s.dis_fun~all_permanova_fun$Soil_Horizon + all_permanova_fun$Forest_Stand, permutations = 999)


perform_pairwise_adonis <- function(otu_table, mapping, factor_name, sim_method = "bray", p_adjust_method = "bonferroni", table_title = NULL) {
  # Replace dots with hyphens in row names
  rownames(mapping) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(mapping))
  rownames(otu_table) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(otu_table))
  
  # Combine OTU table and mapping table by ID
  all_permanova <- left_join(rownames_to_column(otu_table, "ID"), 
                             rownames_to_column(mapping, "ID"), by = "ID")
  
  # Ensure the factor column exists in the mapping table
  if (!(factor_name %in% colnames(mapping))) {
    stop(paste("Factor", factor_name, "not found in the metadata table."))
  }
  
  # Run pairwise.adonis for the given factor
  pairwise_results <- pairwiseAdonis::pairwise.adonis(
    all_permanova[, 2:(ncol(otu_table) + 1)],  # OTU data part
    all_permanova[[factor_name]],              # Factor column for grouping
    sim.method = sim_method,                   # Dissimilarity method (e.g., "bray")
    p.adjust.m = p_adjust_method               # P-value adjustment method
  )
  
  # Print and return the results
  print(pairwise_results)
  
  # Display the pairwise results in a table format using kable
  pairwise_results_df <- as.data.frame(pairwise_results)
  
  # Use custom title if provided, otherwise default to factor name
  caption_text <- ifelse(is.null(table_title), 
                         paste("Pairwise Adonis Results for", factor_name), 
                         table_title)
  
  # Format the table for HTML display in RMarkdown
formatted_table <- pairwise_results_df %>%
  knitr::kable(format = "html", caption = caption_text) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"))

# Print the table so it appears in the report
print(formatted_table)

invisible(list(data = pairwise_results_df, table = formatted_table))

}

pairwise_results_fun <- perform_pairwise_adonis(
  otu_table = otu_hell_fun, 
  mapping = metadata, 
  factor_name = "Soil_Horizon",
  table_title = "Fungi: Permanova results for level Soil_Horizon"
)
#export this table

pairwise_results_test <- perform_pairwise_adonis(
  otu_table = otu_hell_fun, 
  mapping = metadata, 
  factor_name = "Forest_Stand",
  table_title = "Fungi: Permanova results for level Forest_Stand"
)
#export this table

pairwise_results_fun <- perform_pairwise_adonis(
  otu_table = otu_hell_fun, 
  mapping = metadata, 
  factor_name = "Country",
  table_title = "Fungi: Permanova results for level Country"
)
#export this table

```
##Relative Abundance- tijana
```{r}
# Helper function to calculate relative abundance using phyloseq

calculate_relative_abundance <- function(physeq_obj) {
  transform_sample_counts(physeq_obj, function(x) { x / sum(x) })
}

# Helper function to prune taxa based on given top taxa using phyloseq
prune_top_taxa <- function(physeq_obj, tax_rank, top_taxa) {
  prune_taxa(tax_table(physeq_obj)[, tax_rank] %in% top_taxa, physeq_obj)
}

# Helper function to generate relative abundance plots, with ggplot, can be customized 
generate_relative_abundance_plot <- function(data, x_var, y_var, fill_var, title, color_values) {
  ggplot(data, aes_string(x = x_var, y = y_var, fill = fill_var)) + 
    theme_bw() +
    geom_bar(stat = 'identity', position = "fill", width = .8) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 14, face = "bold"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
          strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 18, face = "italic"),
          legend.position = "right",
          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
          plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1, 1, 1), "cm")) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_values) +
    labs(x = "", y = "Relative abundance", title = title) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 1)) +
    facet_grid(~Country+Forest_Stand, drop = TRUE, scales = "free", space = "free", labeller = label_wrap_gen(width = 15))
}

# Create phyloseq object

otu_fin <- otu_table(otutab_filtered_fun, taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
tax_fin <- tax_table(as.matrix(tax_filtered_fun))
mapping_fin <- sample_data(metadata)

finmer <- phyloseq(otu_fin, tax_fin, mapping_fin) #this will be used for beta diversity
finmer


# Calculate relative abundance (function we made)
finmer_relabund <- transform_sample_counts(finmer, function(x) { x / sum(x) })

#Melt the data - this transforms the phyloseq grande table to R data frame (a big, long table with all information that we need)
finmer_melt <- psmelt(finmer_relabund)

# Calculate total sums for genus (sum of raw sequence reads, to see which taxa are most abundant accross the dataset), we combine all OTUs of the same Genus

finmer_sum <- tapply(taxa_sums(finmer), tax_table(finmer)[, "Genus"], sum, na.rm = TRUE)

finmer_sum

# Look at the finmer_sum under Values in the Environment, you will see that there is 1239 genera in total, we will select 20 most abundant ones to plot, and put the rest into "Others"; 1239 is impossible to visualise and also not very informative

# Select top genera
top_genus <- names(sort(finmer_sum, TRUE))[1:20] # select top 20 genera
other_genus <- names(sort(finmer_sum, TRUE))[21:1412] #this is the rest

# Prune to top genera - create datasets with both top and other genera, take relative abundance phyloseq 

top_genus_prune <- prune_top_taxa(finmer_relabund, "Genus", top_genus)
other_genus_prune <- prune_top_taxa(finmer_relabund, "Genus", other_genus)

# Melt the data - this transforms the phyloseq grande table to R data frame (a big, long table with all information that we need), we have two tables that we will later combine
finmer_top_genus_melted <- psmelt(top_genus_prune)
finmer_other_genus_melted <- psmelt(other_genus_prune)

# Combine and adjust data
finmer_other_genus_melted$Genus_phylum <- "Other fungi" # All the genera that are in "other" phyloseq will be now renamed to "Other fungi"
finmer_combined <- rbind(finmer_top_genus_melted, finmer_other_genus_melted)

# finmer_combined now has the data you need for the plotting, but we will still group by columns that we need and calculate average relative abundance per group

# Aggregate data for plotting
colnames(finmer_combined)

finmer_aggregate <- finmer_combined %>%
  group_by(OTU, Sample, Genus, Genus_phylum, Phylum, Ecology_new, Soil_Horizon, Forest_Stand, Country) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  mutate(Relative_abundance = Abundance / sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Rename columns if needed
# colnames(finmer_aggregate) <- c("Sample_name", "Site_ID", "Genus", "Genus_phylum", "Phylum", "Ecology", "Compartment", "Forest_management", "Abundance", "Relative_abundance")

# Relevel factors - this way Other fungi always shows first with is nicer for the graph
finmer_aggregate$Genus_phylum = relevel(factor(finmer_aggregate$Genus_phylum), "Other fungi") 

# This code checks that your calculations are OK - everything should be summed up to 1 (this is same as 100%), each sample
finmer_aggregate %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(Relative_abundance)) %>%
  summary()

# If you need to extract relative abundance values for whatever reason, this code can be used
# 
# relabund_wide <- finmer_aggregate %>%
#   select(Genus_phylum, Compartment, Relative_abundance) %>%
#   group_by(Compartment, Genus_phylum) %>%
#   dplyr::summarise(Relative_abundance = sum(Relative_abundance)*100, .groups="drop") %>%
#   pivot_wider(names_from = Genus_phylum, values_from = Relative_abundance, values_fill = 0)
# 
# relabund_percent_view <- relabund_wide %>%
#   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ formatC(.x * 100, format = "f", digits = 4)))
# 
# head(relabund_percent_view)

# Plot relative abundance

finmer_aggregate$Soil_Horizon <- factor (finmer_aggregate$Soil_Horizon, 
                                                      levels = c("FH",
                                                                 "1",
                                                                 "2", "3", "4"))



genus_plot <- generate_relative_abundance_plot(finmer_aggregate, "Soil_Horizon", "Relative_abundance", "Genus_phylum", "Relative Abundance by Genus", color_values_genus)

genus_ecology_plot <- generate_relative_abundance_plot(finmer_aggregate, "Soil_Horizon", "Relative_abundance", "Genus_eco", "Relative Abundance by Genus", color_values_genus)
unique(finmer_aggregate$Ecology_new)

finmer_aggregate$Ecology_new <- factor (finmer_aggregate$Ecology_new, 
                                                       levels = c("Unknown", "Others", "ErM", "EcM", "Plant pathogen", "Saprotroph (other)", "Saprotroph (litter)", "Saprotroph (soil)", "Saprotroph (wood)"))

ecology_plot <- generate_relative_abundance_plot(finmer_aggregate, "Soil_Horizon", "Relative_abundance", "Ecology_new", "Relative Abundance by Guild", color_values_eco)

print(genus_plot)
print(ecology_plot)

ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/relativeabundance/genus_plot.png",plot=genus_plot,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")
ggsave("C:/Users/reves/OneDrive - CZU v Praze/2029RevesDissertationMaterials/2029Reves_Soil_eDNA_specific_documents/data_analysis/Hoffmeister_DeepSoil_Fungi/R_outputs/betadiversity/relativeabundance/ecology_plot.png",plot=ecology_plot,width = 14, height = 8, units = "in")
```
